scope: Introduce a closed-world, exact-identity OpenAI structured-output envelope for generic llm-check reports while retaining the unchanged canonical S04 semantic gate and every non-OpenAI structured-schema path.

test_results.command: isolated deterministic first-pass: bin/sworn verify --verifier-model openai-completions/s21-proof-gate --spec docs/release/2026-07-15-baton-v0.15-conformance/S21-openai-structured-envelope/spec.json --diff - --proof docs/release/2026-07-15-baton-v0.15-conformance/S21-openai-structured-envelope/proof.json

reachability.evidence: cmd/sworn/llmcheck_test.go:TestLLMCheckOpenAIResponsesStructuredEnvelopeBinaryReachability and TestLLMCheckOpenAICompletionsStructuredEnvelopeBinaryReachability build and invoke the public sworn llm-check --type ac-satisfaction command against deterministic local Responses text.format and chat/completions response_format endpoints. Each fake inspects the named envelope, returns a real model-shaped ac-satisfaction PASS object, and the built binary exits 0.

delivered.item: AC-01: Only exact canonical llm-check-report-v1 bytes select the sealed llm-check-report-v1-openai-envelope on explicitly profiled OpenAI Responses text.format and chat/completions response_format wires.
AC-02: Direct and proxy construction carry explicit provider profile and wire mode for both retained OpenAI paths, including the deprecated Responses alias and deterministic base-URL override.

AC-03: Digest mismatches, future generic-report-family IDs, and the dedicated spec-ambiguity report reject locally before dispatch with deterministic source-free errors and zero fake-endpoint requests.

AC-04 and AC-05: The generic gate continues to receive the canonical source bytes and locally rejects canonical semantic violations or emitted check mismatch; the adapter cannot synthesize or repair a report.
AC-04: xAI native response_format, forced tool-call, and unprofiled OAI-compatible clients retain their supplied-schema behavior and cannot select the envelope by URL or concrete type.

AC-06: The proof uses deterministic local transport evidence only; the live diff contains no canonical vendored schema, prompt, or internal/gate/llmcheck.go source change, and S20 remains blocked pending a fresh S21 verifier PASS.
Intentional local fake HTTP test transport is declared with @mock-boundary comments, and the slice mock lint passes without exempting production behavior.
delivered.evidence: cmd/sworn/llmcheck_test.go; internal/gate/llmcheck_test.go; internal/model/oai_test.go; bin/sworn lint mock --slice S21-openai-structured-envelope --release 2026-07-15-baton-v0.15-conformance --base ed0badf68673f0af84834458f07be0792555484f

not_delivered.item: Credentialed OpenAI acceptance smoke and S20 readiness and maintainability reruns.
not_delivered.why: S21 proves only the deterministic provider-envelope boundary. A credentialed emitted result remains S20 evidence after a fresh S21 verifier PASS, so no local fake endpoint is represented as S20 evidence.
not_delivered.tracking: S20-v015-parity-portable-fixture after fresh S21-openai-structured-envelope verifier PASS.

divergence.description: internal/model/structured.go was an anticipated touchpoint but needed no edit: the new closed-world compiler selects the envelope before calling the existing strictProjection, intentionally preserving that generic projection unchanged for every non-selected source and provider.
